feature: Replace TEST TAKSTATUS mock with real 84000 Status drift
summary: TAKSTATUS is a COMPUTED status: the 'Uppstart/Avstängning 84000-CM1' program decides from the valve gates whether the roof pump should run (OR = any valve open -> on; all off -> off). Today it writes that result to a MOCK boolean, Tasks\Kylvatten anläggning tak 84000-CM1\TEST TAKSTATUS. Repoint it to the real point, Devices\Lora-Modbus\Slave\84000 Status drift, so the computed status drives the actual signal - and point the Takstatus dashboard tile at the same real object, then delete the mock. Each step is checked against your .bos and badged; status reflects the exported snapshot, so re-export and reload after editing in the Configurator. Diffs are previews only; NOTHING is written to bOS.

steps.title: Confirm the real target exists
steps.detail: This is the real roof-pump status point on the LoRa-Modbus slave (DPTBoolean, Address 7). The program will WRITE the computed status here instead of to the mock. Heads-up: this DPT has ReadCyclically = true - if the slave does not echo back exactly what you write, the cyclic poll can overwrite your computed value. If 84000 Status drift is purely the point you drive (not a register the device reports independently), uncheck ReadCyclically so the poll doesn't fight the SetValue.

highlight.warn: Modbus Table = Inputs -> read-only; a SetValue here won't reach the device. Confirm this point is writable before relying on it.

steps.title: Retarget the program's writes to the real point
steps.detail: Open this Program. In its Commands, change both SetValue targets from 'Tasks\Kylvatten anläggning tak 84000-CM1\TEST TAKSTATUS' to 'Devices\Lora-Modbus\Slave\84000 Status drift' (keep the values: True under OR gate = True, False under AND gate = False). The conditions stay as-is; only the object being written changes. NOTE: the off-branch keys off AND gate = False ('not all valves on'), not OR gate = False ('all valves off'), which contradicts the 'on while ANY valve open' rule. Now that it drives the real pump, fix that branch to OR gate = False (or drop it) so the pump isn't forced off whenever just one valve is closed.

all.label: 84000 Status drift = True under OR gate = True

steps.title: Repoint the Takstatus dashboard tile
steps.detail: This tile is the only other consumer of the mock. Change its bound object from 'Tasks\Kylvatten anläggning tak 84000-CM1\TEST TAKSTATUS.Value' to 'Devices\Lora-Modbus\Slave\84000 Status drift.Value' so it shows the same real status the program now drives.

all.label: Takstatus tile now binds to 84000 Status drift

steps.title: Delete the orphaned TEST TAKSTATUS node
steps.detail: Once the program writes (step 2) and the tile (step 3) point at 84000 Status drift, nothing references TEST TAKSTATUS. Confirm its Usages tab is empty, then delete the Variable.Boolean node so the mock is fully removed. If Usages still lists anything, finish that reference first - don't delete a node that is still bound.

steps.title: Re-export and reload to re-grade
steps.detail: Export the updated GB1625-Building.bos from the Configurator and reload it in the Co-pilot. Steps 1-3 should badge green; the mock node should be gone from the tree. Then live-test: open one machine valve and confirm 84000 Status drift goes True and the Takstatus tile follows; close the last valve and confirm it goes False.
